summary: Iteration 116 — Verified Admin Bookings fix (limit cap raise from 200 -> 1000) + support_tickets team-members endpoints after duplicate cleanup. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests PASS. Frontend smoke: /admin/bookings now loads with All=163, Online=154, Walk-in=9 for admin (matches task spec exactly); all three toolbar filters (operator-scope, quick-date-range, view-mode) render; booking rows visible in list view.

minor.issue: Frontend sends limit=500; backend now caps at le=1000 (was le=200 causing 422). Fix verified. Recommend frontend align by either lowering to 200 or keeping 500 explicitly documented.

ui_bugs.issue: React console warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop. Check the render method of AdminBookings.' Non-blocking but should be fixed by adding stable key={booking.id || booking.order_id} to the mapped list.

integration_issues.issue: 2x 422 errors logged in console during login page init (before credentials entered). Not related to this iteration but worth a look — likely a pre-auth API call firing with missing params on the splash screen.

action_items: Optional: add stable `key` prop in AdminBookings list render to clear the React key warning.
Optional: investigate the 2x 422 errors on /login splash (fires before login submit) — unrelated to this iteration but present in console.
critical_code_review_comments: manual_bookings.py line 308: limit Query(50, ge=1, le=1000) — correct. Matches frontend's limit=500 call with headroom.

support_tickets.py: duplicates cleanup verified — only ONE definition each of get_team_members (L841), add_team_member (L1015), remove_team_member (L1050). File length 1536 lines, `grep -c 'def get_team_members|def add_team_member|def remove_team_member'` returns 3. No shadowing risk.
support_tickets.py add_team_member requires `department` and `type` fields (employee/admin/user) — frontend CustomerServiceManagement.jsx should ensure both are provided in POST payload.
Bookings.jsx limit=500 — acceptable given the 1000 cap. If bookings volume grows >1000, pagination on frontend becomes necessary (currently single-shot fetch).
No changes needed to other iterations (113/115) — regressions not re-run but scope is isolated to the two files edited.

success_rate.frontend: PASS — /admin/bookings page loads 163 bookings with all toolbar filters; one non-blocking React key warning

test_credentials: admin@test.com / superadmin@oryno.com / operator@test.com / customer@test.com — all testpassword123
seed_data_creation: Created/deleted 1 TEST_iter116_* support team member per test run (clean teardown within test_add_and_remove_team_member).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_iteration116_admin_bookings.py covers: (1) GET /api/operator/manual-bookings/ with limit=500/1000/1001 for admin/super_admin/operator, channel filter on_site/online, and (2) full CRUD on /api/support-tickets/team-members. Admin sees 163 bookings, operator sees 26 (scoped), super_admin sees 163 — matches task spec. SupportTeamMemberAdd model requires id, name, email, role, department, type.

feature_verdict.frontend_admin_bookings_renders: PASS (163/154/9 counts shown, all 3 toolbar filters present, list rows visible)

rca of the issue: Root cause: GET /api/operator/manual-bookings/ had limit capped at le=200 via Pydantic Query validator (manual_bookings.py:308), while frontend Bookings.jsx line 67 calls with limit=500. This caused FastAPI to return 422 'Input should be less than or equal to 200', aborting the fetch and leaving the page showing 0 records. Fix: raised cap to le=1000. Verified: admin=163, operator=26 (scoped), super_admin=163 records returned successfully; page renders list rows and stats chips (All 163 / Online 154 / Walk-in 9).
